community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_binding.description: This module uses rabbitMQ REST APIs to create / delete bindings.

state.description: Whether the bindings should be present or absent.

name.description: source exchange to create binding on.

destination.description: destination exchange or queue for the binding.

arguments.description: extra arguments for exchange. If defined this argument is a key/value dictionary

community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_exchange.description: This module uses rabbitMQ Rest API to create/delete exchanges

name.description: Name of the exchange to create.

state.description: Whether the exchange should be present or absent.

durable.description: Whether exchange is durable or not.

exchange_type.description: Type for the exchange. If using I(x-delayed-message), I(x-random), I(x-consistent-hash) or I(x-recent-history) the respective plugin on the RabbitMQ server must be enabled.

auto_delete.description: If the exchange should delete itself after all queues/exchanges unbound from it.

internal.description: Exchange is available only for other exchanges.

arguments.description: Extra arguments for exchange. If defined this argument is a key/value dictionary.

community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_feature_flag.description: Allows to enable specified feature flag.

node.description: Erlang node name of the target rabbit node.

community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_global_param.description: Manage dynamic, cluster-wide global parameters for RabbitMQ

name.description: Name of the global parameter being set

value.description: Value of the global parameter, as a JSON term

node.description: erlang node name of the rabbit we wish to configure

state.description: Specify if global parameter is to be added or removed

community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_parameter.description: Manage dynamic, cluster-wide parameters for RabbitMQ

component.description: Name of the component of which the parameter is being set

name.description: Name of the parameter being set

value.description: Value of the parameter, as a JSON term

node.description: erlang node name of the rabbit we wish to configure

state.description: Specify if parameter is to be added or removed

community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_plugin.description: This module can be used to enable or disable RabbitMQ plugins.

names.description: Comma-separated list of plugin names. Also, accepts plugin name.

new_only.description: Only enable missing plugins. Does not disable plugins that are not in the names list.

state.description: Specify if plugins are to be enabled or disabled.

broker_state.description: Specify whether the broker should be online or offline for the plugin change.

prefix.description: Specify a custom install prefix to a Rabbit.

community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_policy.short_description: Manage the state of policies in RabbitMQ
community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_policy.description: Manage the state of a policy in RabbitMQ using rabbitmqctl or REST APIs.

name.description: The name of the policy to manage.

vhost.description: The name of the vhost to apply to.

apply_to.description: What the policy applies to. Requires RabbitMQ 3.2.0 or later. For classic_queues, quorum_queues and streams RabbitMQ 3.12 or later is required

pattern.description: A regex of queues to apply the policy to. Required when C(state=present). This option is no longer required as of Ansible 2.9.

tags.description: A dict or string describing the policy. Required when C(state=present). This option is no longer required as of Ansible 2.9.

node.description: Erlang node name of the rabbit we wish to configure.

ca_cert.description: CA certificate to verify SSL connection to management API.

client_cert.description: Client certificate to send on SSL connections to management API.

client_key.description: Private key matching the client certificate.

community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_publish.short_description: Publish a message to a RabbitMQ queue.
community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_publish.description: Publish a message on a RabbitMQ queue using a blocking connection.

url.description: An URL connection string to connect to the RabbitMQ server. I(url) and I(host)/I(port)/I(user)/I(pass)/I(vhost) are mutually exclusive, use either or but not both.

host.description: The RabbitMQ server hostname or IP.

vhost.description: The virtual host to target. If default vhost is required, use C('%2F').

queue.description: The queue to publish a message to.  If no queue is specified, RabbitMQ will return a random queue name. A C(queue) cannot be provided if an C(exchange) is specified.

exchange.description: The exchange to publish a message to. An C(exchange) cannot be provided if a C(queue) is specified.

body.description: The body of the message. A C(body) cannot be provided if a C(src) is specified.

src.description: A file to upload to the queue.  Automatic mime type detection is attempted if content_type is not defined (left as default). A C(src) cannot be provided if a C(body) is specified. The filename is added to the headers of the posted message to RabbitMQ. Key being the C(filename), value is the filename.

content_type.description: The content type of the body.

durable.description: Set the queue to be durable.

exclusive.description: Set the queue to be exclusive.

auto_delete.description: Set the queue to auto delete.

headers.description: A dictionary of headers to post with the message.

cafile.description: CA file used during connection to the RabbitMQ server over SSL. If this option is specified, also I(certfile) and I(keyfile) must be specified.

certfile.description: Client certificate to establish SSL connection. If this option is specified, also I(cafile) and I(keyfile) must be specified.

keyfile.description: Client key to establish SSL connection. If this option is specified, also I(cafile) and I(certfile) must be specified.

community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_queue.description: This module uses rabbitMQ Rest API to create/delete queues. Due to limitations in the API, it cannot modify existing queues.

state.description: Whether the queue should be present or absent.

durable.description: whether queue is durable or not.

auto_delete.description: if the queue should delete itself after all queues/queues unbound from it.

message_ttl.description: How long a message can live in queue before it is discarded (milliseconds).

auto_expires.description: How long a queue can be unused before it is automatically deleted (milliseconds).

max_length.description: How many messages can the queue contain before it starts rejecting.

dead_letter_exchange.description: Optional name of an exchange to which messages will be republished if they are rejected or expire.

dead_letter_routing_key.description: Optional replacement routing key to use when a message is dead-lettered. Original routing key will be used if unset.

max_priority.description: Maximum number of priority levels for the queue to support. If not set, the queue will not support message priorities. Larger numbers indicate higher priority.

arguments.description: extra arguments for queue. If defined this argument is a key/value dictionary Arguments here take precedence over parameters. If both are defined, the argument will be used.

node.description: Erlang node name of the target rabbit node.

community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_user.description: Add or remove users to RabbitMQ and assign permissions

password.description: Password of user to add. To change the password of an existing user, you must also specify C(update_password=always).

tags.description: User tags specified as comma delimited. The suggested tags to use are management, policymaker, monitoring and administrator.

permissions.description: a list of dicts, each dict contains vhost, configure_priv, write_priv, and read_priv, and represents a permission rule for that vhost. This option should be preferable when you care about all permissions of the user. You should use vhost, configure_priv, write_priv, and read_priv options instead if you care about permissions for just some vhosts.

vhost.description: vhost to apply access privileges. This option will be ignored when permissions option is used.

node.description: erlang node name of the rabbit we wish to configure

configure_priv.description: Regular expression to restrict configure actions on a resource for the specified vhost. By default all actions are restricted. This option will be ignored when permissions option is used.

write_priv.description: Regular expression to restrict configure actions on a resource for the specified vhost. By default all actions are restricted. This option will be ignored when permissions option is used.

read_priv.description: Regular expression to restrict configure actions on a resource for the specified vhost. By default all actions are restricted. This option will be ignored when permissions option is used.

topic_permissions.description: A list of dicts, each dict contains vhost, exchange, read_priv and write_priv, and represents a topic permission rule for that vhost. By default vhost is C(/) and exchange is C(amq.topic). Supported since RabbitMQ 3.7.0. If RabbitMQ is older and topic_permissions are set, the module will fail.

state.description: Specify if user is to be added or removed

update_password.description: C(on_create) will only set the password for newly created users.  C(always) will update passwords if they differ.

login_protocol.description: Specify which TCP/IP protocol will be used.

login_password.description: Login password of the management API.

community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_user_limits.description: Manage the state of user limits in RabbitMQ. Supported since RabbitMQ version 3.8.10.

user.description: Name of user to manage limits for.

max_connections.description: Max number of concurrent client connections. Negative value means "no limit". Ignored when the I(state) is C(absent).

max_channels.description: Max number of channels. Negative value means "no limit". Ignored when the I(state) is C(absent).

node.description: Name of the RabbitMQ Erlang node to manage.

state.description: Specify whether the limits are to be set or cleared. If set to C(absent), the limits of both I(max_connections) and I(max_channels) will be cleared.

community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_vhost.short_description: Manage the state of a virtual host in RabbitMQ
community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_vhost.description: Manage the state of a virtual host in RabbitMQ using rabbitmqctl or REST APIs.

name.description: The name of the vhost to manage

node.description: erlang node name of the rabbit we wish to configure

ca_cert.description: CA certificate to verify SSL connection to management API.

client_cert.description: Client certificate to send on SSL connections to management API.

client_key.description: Private key matching the client certificate.

community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_vhost_limits.short_description: Manage the state of virtual host limits in RabbitMQ
community.rabbitmq.rabbitmq_vhost_limits.description: This module sets/clears certain limits on a virtual host. The configurable limits are I(max_connections) and I(max-queues).

max_connections.description: Max number of concurrent client connections. Negative value means "no limit". Ignored when the I(state) is C(absent).

max_queues.description: Max number of queues. Negative value means "no limit". Ignored when the I(state) is C(absent).

node.description: Name of the RabbitMQ Erlang node to manage.

state.description: Specify whether the limits are to be set or cleared. If set to C(absent), the limits of both I(max_connections) and I(max-queues) will be cleared.

vhost.description: Name of the virtual host to manage.
